title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal risk, but improper implementation or over-reliance on automated tools could lead to missed vulnerabilities or false positives. Additionally, if the tool contains undiscovered flaws, it could potentially be exploited by malicious actors.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment before deploying it in production systems.
Regularly update the tool to ensure it includes the latest vulnerability databases and patches.
Use the tool as part of a broader security strategy, combining it with manual code reviews and other security measures.

title: Nanobot prior to version 0.2.1 contains a denial of service vulnerability in the Matrix channel...
description: Nanobot prior to version 0.2.1 contains a denial of service vulnerability in the Matrix channel media download handler that allows authenticated room members to exhaust process memory and bandwidth by sending media events with missing or invalid size metadata. Attackers can send multiple concurrent Matrix media events with omitted or invalid declared sizes to trigger simultaneous large media downloads that fully materialize response bodies before post-download rejection, consuming process resour

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to exhaust system memory and bandwidth by exploiting the media download handler in Nanobot's Matrix channel, potentially leading to denial of service and disruption of operations.
recommended_actions: Update Nanobot to version 0.2.1 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement rate limiting and resource allocation controls to prevent excessive media downloads.
Monitor system logs for unusual activity related to Matrix media events and block suspicious users.

title: A flaw was found in gnutls. The PKCS#7 padding check, performed during decryption, was not...
description: A flaw was found in gnutls. The PKCS#7 padding check, performed during decryption, was not constant-time. This timing side-channel could allow a remote attacker to potentially leak sensitive information about the padding bytes through observable timing differences. This vulnerability is a form of information disclosure.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5419.

title: The DeepAI endpoint 'https://api.deepai.org/change_user_email' accepts POST requests without any...
description: The DeepAI endpoint 'https://api.deepai.org/change_user_email' accepts POST requests without any CSRF protection. If an attacker can trick a logged-in user into clicking a malicious link, the attacker can change the user's email address and take over their account. Fixed on 2026-05-20.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-49433.

title: Nanobot prior to version 0.2.1 contains a server-side request forgery vulnerability in the...
description: Nanobot prior to version 0.2.1 contains a server-side request forgery vulnerability in the Microsoft Teams channel handler that allows remote attackers to exfiltrate Bot Framework bearer tokens by supplying a forged activity with an attacker-controlled serviceUrl value. Attackers can poison the stored conversation reference by sending a crafted inbound activity to the Teams webhook, causing subsequent bot replies to transmit token-bearing Authorization header requests to an attacker-controlled h

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-49139.

title: CodexBar prior to 0.32.0 contains an insecure temporary file handling vulnerability that allows...
description: CodexBar prior to 0.32.0 contains an insecure temporary file handling vulnerability that allows local attackers to access sensitive credentials or tamper with build artifacts by exploiting predictable file paths in the release notarization workflow. Attackers with access to the same host can read the App Store Connect API key written to a fixed path, pre-create files or symbolic links at predictable locations to redirect writes to attacker-controlled destinations, or tamper with notarization arc

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in CodexBar prior to version 0.32.0 allows local attackers to exploit insecure temporary file handling, leading to unauthorized access to sensitive credentials (e.g., App Store Connect API keys) or tampering with build artifacts. This could compromise the integrity of software releases and lead to further exploitation or data breaches.
recommended_actions: Upgrade CodexBar to version 0.32.0 or later, as the vulnerability has been addressed in this release.
Implement stricter access controls and permissions on the host system to limit unauthorized local access.
Review and secure workflows that handle sensitive credentials or temporary files, ensuring unpredictable file paths and proper cleanup.

title: A vulnerability was identified in code-projects Hotel and Tourism Reservation System 1.0. This...
description: A vulnerability was identified in code-projects Hotel and Tourism Reservation System 1.0. This issue affects the function password_verify of the file /admin/login.php of the component Admin Login. Such manipulation of the argument Password leads to improper authentication. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Hotel and Tourism Reservation System 1.0 allows attackers to bypass authentication via improper handling of the password_verify function. This could result in unauthorized access to administrative functions, exposing sensitive customer data and potentially disrupting operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available patches or updates provided by the software vendor to address CVE-2026-10288.
Implement strong access controls and monitor admin login activity for unusual behavior to detect potential exploitation attempts.
Conduct a security review of the system to identify and mitigate other potential vulnerabilities.

title: Nanobot prior to version 0.2.1 contains a server-side request forgery vulnerability in the...
description: Nanobot prior to version 0.2.1 contains a server-side request forgery vulnerability in the web_fetch tool that allows remote attackers to reach internal or private network hosts by supplying a URL that redirects to a loopback or private address via a 3xx Location header. Attackers can exploit the automatic HTTP redirect following behavior in the httpx library to bypass initial URL validation and cause the runtime to send outbound requests to internal hosts before final resolved URL validation is

title: Banana Slides through 0.4.0, patched in commit e8bc490, contains a path traversal vulnerability...
description: Banana Slides through 0.4.0, patched in commit e8bc490, contains a path traversal vulnerability in the generate_image() function within the AI service backend that allows unauthenticated attackers to read arbitrary image-format files outside the intended uploads directory by exploiting an incomplete path prefix check using os.path.startswith() without a trailing separator. Attackers can supply crafted markdown image references in user-controlled page descriptions that resolve to sibling director

title: A vulnerability was determined in SourceCodester SEO Meta Tag Extractor 1.0. This vulnerability...
description: A vulnerability was determined in SourceCodester SEO Meta Tag Extractor 1.0. This vulnerability affects the function get_headers of the file /index.php. This manipulation of the argument url causes server-side request forgery. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in SourceCodester SEO Meta Tag Extractor 1.0 allows for server-side request forgery (SSRF), which can be exploited remotely. Attackers could potentially access internal systems, steal sensitive data, or use the server as a proxy for further attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization for the 'url' parameter in the get_headers function to prevent malicious inputs.
Restrict outgoing server requests to only trusted and necessary destinations by implementing network-level egress filtering.
Monitor and patch the application with the latest security updates or consider upgrading to a newer, secure version if available.

title: A vulnerability has been found in DevaslanPHP project-management up to 2.0.0-beta1. Affected by...
description: A vulnerability has been found in DevaslanPHP project-management up to 2.0.0-beta1. Affected by this issue is the function KanbanScrumHelper::recordUpdated of the file app/Helpers/KanbanScrumHelper.php of the component Ticket Handler. The manipulation leads to improper authorization. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

title: CodexBar prior to 0.32.0 contains a privilege escalation vulnerability in the CLI installer that...
description: CodexBar prior to 0.32.0 contains a privilege escalation vulnerability in the CLI installer that allows local attackers to execute arbitrary commands as root by exploiting a race condition in temporary file handling. The installer creates a temporary file with mktemp, writes a privileged shell payload into it, and executes it with administrator privileges via bash, allowing a same-user local process to rewrite the installer body before the administrator prompt is approved, causing attacker-contr

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The privilege escalation vulnerability in CodexBar prior to version 0.32.0 allows local attackers to gain root access by exploiting a race condition in temporary file handling during the CLI installation process. This could lead to unauthorized access, system compromise, and potential data breaches.
recommended_actions: Immediately update CodexBar to version 0.32.0 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement stricter file handling practices, such as using secure temporary file creation methods and avoiding execution of files in writable directories.
Conduct a security audit of systems where CodexBar is installed to identify any signs of exploitation.

title: FlexRIC v2.0.0 allows a single SCTP connection to bind multiple xapp_ids by sending multiple...
description: FlexRIC v2.0.0 allows a single SCTP connection to bind multiple xapp_ids by sending multiple E42_SETUP_REQUESTs. On disconnect, only the first registered xapp_id's resources are cleaned up; subsequent xapp_ids and their subscriptions remain as stale entries. A remote attacker can exploit this to leak subscription state in the iApp, potentially causing resource exhaustion or state corruption over time.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in FlexRIC v2.0.0 allows attackers to exploit improper cleanup of xapp_id resources, leading to potential resource exhaustion and state corruption. This could disrupt system operations and degrade performance over time, especially in environments with high traffic or critical real-time applications.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest patch or update from the FlexRIC maintainers to address the issue (if available).
Implement monitoring and alerting mechanisms to detect unusual resource usage or stale subscriptions.
Restrict access to the SCTP connection to trusted entities and enforce strict authentication and authorization controls.

title: IBM WebSphere Application Server 9.0, and 8.5 is vulnerable to identity spoofing.
description: IBM WebSphere Application Server 9.0, and 8.5 is vulnerable to identity spoofing.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in IBM WebSphere Application Server 9.0 and 8.5 allows attackers to perform identity spoofing, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data breaches, and privilege escalation.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest security patches provided by IBM to address CVE-2026-8644.
Review and monitor access logs for any signs of unauthorized access or identity spoofing.
Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) and review identity management policies to mitigate potential exploitation.

title: IBM WebSphere Application Server 9.0, and 8.5 is affected by an improper validation of user...
description: IBM WebSphere Application Server 9.0, and 8.5 is affected by an improper validation of user-supplied data during deserialization using the SAML Web Single Sign-On component. This could result in remote code execution via a crafted HTTP request when combined with a suitable gadget chain.

title: A NULL pointer dereference in the ext4_dir_en_get_name_len function in include/ext4_dir.h of...
description: A NULL pointer dereference in the ext4_dir_en_get_name_len function in include/ext4_dir.h of lwext4 1.0.0 allows attackers to cause a denial of service by supplying a specially crafted EXT4 filesystem image with malformed directory entries. During directory iteration, the code may fail to validate the directory entry pointer before accessing the name_len field, resulting in a segmentation fault. This affects versions based on (or equivalent to) the 2016-era codebase (1.0.0).

title: Deserialization of untrusted data in Microsoft Office SharePoint allows an authorized attacker to...
description: Deserialization of untrusted data in Microsoft Office SharePoint allows an authorized attacker to execute code over a network.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an authorized attacker to execute arbitrary code over a network by exploiting deserialization of untrusted data in Microsoft Office SharePoint. This can lead to data breaches, unauthorized access, and potential lateral movement within the network.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches and updates provided by Microsoft for SharePoint to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict access to SharePoint servers to only trusted users and implement strict access controls.
Monitor network traffic and logs for any suspicious activity or unauthorized access attempts targeting SharePoint.

title: CVE-2026-8652 - An OS Command Injection vulnerability exists in Aterm. If a malicious third person gains administrat
description: An OS Command Injection vulnerability exists in Aterm. If a malicious third person gains administrator access to the product’s web console, they may be able to execute arbitrary OS commands via adjacent network.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8652.

title: CVE-2026-6059 - A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists in Aterm. Arbitrary scripts may be executed in the web b
description: A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists in Aterm. Arbitrary scripts may be executed in the web browser of a user accessing the web management interface via adjacent network.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers on the same network to execute arbitrary scripts in the web browser of users accessing the web management interface of Aterm devices. This could lead to session hijacking, data theft, or unauthorized actions within the management interface.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest firmware update or patch provided by the vendor to address the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the web management interface by enabling network segmentation and ensuring it is only accessible from trusted devices.
Educate users on avoiding untrusted networks and monitoring for unusual activity in the management interface.

title: CVE-2026-9489 - NitroSense 3.x before 3.01.3052 contains Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability.The program
description: NitroSense 3.x before 3.01.3052 contains Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability.The program exposes a Windows Named Pipe that uses a custom protocol to invoke internal functions. However, this Named Pipe is misconfigured, allowing any authenticated local user to execute arbitrary code with NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM privileges and to delete arbitrary files with SYSTEM privileges. By leveraging this, an attacker can execute arbitrary code on the target system with elevated privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9489.
